Added tests for clean and modified sample repo cases. Verified focused config/doctor tests: 12 passed. Full Elixir 1.20 gate passed: format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 85 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P1.1.3 Pinned toolchain runner image (digest + SBOM)
description:
**What.** Pinned toolchain runner image (digest + SBOM ref).
**Why.** Reproducibility first: the gate records the IMMUTABLE image digest in
RunSpec/ToolInvocation/provenance. Mutable tags are labels; digest is identity.
**Scope.** Dockerfile for sample-python-runner (Python toolchain + pytest [+ Pi
for live path]); build + record image_ref + image_digest + sbom_ref; ToolchainProfile.
**Acceptance.** Image builds; digest recorded in a ToolchainProfile; gate/canary
use image-baked deps (no network bootstrap by default).
**Refs.** §2, §12.4.
close_reason: Added self-contained sample-python-runner Dockerfile pinned to python@sha256:c33f0bc4364a6881bed1ec0cc2665e6c53c87a43e774aaeab88e6f17af105e4f, baked the sample requirements.lock into the image, recorded image_ref/image_digest/dependency lock digest/cache policy/SBOM ref in toolchains/sample-python-runner/profile.toml, and updated ToolMatrix. Verified docker build produced sha256:18be896c98e13585f4d2701490a5be39126ec1b14d429f72b5707b99516b5548; verified runner executes sample pytest with --network none: 3 passed; focused Elixir tests: 8 passed; full Elixir 1.20 gate passed: format check, compile --warnings-as-errors, 86 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P1.10 — Reviewer-on-dossier
description:
# P1.10 — Reviewer-on-dossier

**What.** A separate reviewer `AgentSession` (different profile/model from the
implementer) that reads ONLY the recorded dossier/artifacts and returns a
structured `Review`, with schema validation, actor-separation Ash policy, and a
`ReviewerHealth` fixture suite + freshness gating.

**Why / background.** Reading the recorded dossier (not the live session) makes
review reproducible and prevents the reviewer from being swayed by undocumented
agent narration. An LLM reviewer can rubber-stamp, so reviewer trust is
CALIBRATED (the same principle as gate canaries) before it is gate-relevant.
Malformed reviewer output fails the gate — it is never interpreted creatively.

**Scope.** reviewer session → structured Review (decision/recommendation/summary/
findings/checks); schema validation; actor/profile ≠ implementer Ash policy;
ReviewerHealth fixtures (complete_green_dossier→accepted; missing_acceptance_
mapping→needs_rework; protected_policy_edit→rejected; malformed_evidence_refs→
rejected) + freshness gating.

**Acceptance.** Malformed review is rejected; reviewer actor/profile ≠
implementer; gate uses a review only when ReviewerHealth is green for the
profile+rubric version.

**Refs.** §19, §22.4 (12), §25 P1.10. Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED.
close_reason: Completed P1.10 child beads 10.1 through 10.3: reviewer-on-dossier session runner, schema validation, actor/profile separation, ReviewerHealth fixture calibration, and freshness gating. Verified during child beads with Elixir 1.20 full test gates plus static checks.

title: P1.10.1 Reviewer session → structured Review
description:
**What.** Reviewer AgentSession (separate profile/model) → structured Review.
**Why.** The reviewer reads ONLY the recorded dossier/artifacts (not the live
session) for reproducibility and to avoid being swayed by undocumented narration.
**Scope.** reviewer role session; read dossier by digest; emit Review JSON
(decision/recommendation/summary/findings/checks); record dossier_sha256 +
rubric_version.
**Acceptance.** Reviewer returns a schema-valid Review tied to the dossier digest.
**Refs.** §19, §22.4 (12).
close_reason: Implemented reviewer-on-dossier session runner: creates reviewer AgentSession, reads dossier.md by content digest, validates structured Review JSON, persists Review with dossier_sha256 and rubric_version, and updates review schema/examples. Verified with Elixir 1.20 full test gate, Credo, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.10.2 Review schema validation + actor-separation policy
description:
**What.** Reviewer schema validation + actor-separation Ash policy.
**Why.** Malformed reviewer output fails the gate — never interpreted creatively.
Ash policies enforce reviewer actor/profile ≠ implementer actor/profile.
**Scope.** validate Review against conveyor.review@1; Ash policy actor/profile
separation; reject malformed output.
**Acceptance.** Malformed review rejected; reviewer == implementer is forbidden.
**Refs.** §19.
close_reason: Implemented Review schema rejection and reviewer/implementer actor separation: malformed reviewer JSON fails validation, Review resource validates reviewer session/profile separation, and RunReviewer preflights separation and marks malformed reviewer sessions failed. Verified with Elixir 1.20 full test gate, Credo, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.10.3 ReviewerHealth fixtures + freshness gating
description:
**What.** ReviewerHealth fixture suite + freshness gating.
**Why.** An LLM reviewer can rubber-stamp, so reviewer trust is calibrated before
it is gate-relevant — the same principle as gate canaries.
**Scope.** labeled fixtures (complete_green_dossier→accepted; missing_acceptance_
mapping→needs_rework; protected_policy_edit→rejected; malformed_evidence_refs→
rejected); ReviewerHealth record per profile+rubric; gate uses a review only when
health is green/fresh else stale_reviewer_health.
**Acceptance.** Reviewer passes the fixture suite; stale health blocks gate use.
**Refs.** §19.
close_reason: Implemented reviewer fixture calibration and freshness gating: labeled fixture suite records ReviewerHealth per profile/rubric/suite, rubber-stamp reviewers fail calibration, and stale/missing/failed health returns blocking reviewer-health findings for gate use. Verified with Elixir 1.20 full test gate, Credo, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.11 — Deterministic gate
description:
# P1.11 — Deterministic gate

**What.** The 14-stage deterministic Slice gate (§17): workspace integrity, diff
scope, observed-risk assessment, policy, secret safety, build/install, tests,
acceptance mapping, contract lock, code-quality delta, RunCheck, provenance,
reviewer aggregation, and canary health freshness — composed so the gate passes
ONLY if all required stages pass, with fail-closed semantics and stop-the-line.

**Why / background.** The gate is the heart of the factory — the human's
stand-in. Phase 1 implements a Slice-level gate, but the stage model must support
later epic/phase gates. If the agent claims success and the conductor cannot
reproduce it, the run fails. RunCheck never "best-effort" parses unknown artifact
versions. Canary freshness is keyed by gate/policy/test-pack/image/quality/
canary-suite digests; a stale green canary is not sufficient.

**Scope (subtasks).** stage framework + composition + fail-closed; stages 1–2
(workspace integrity + diff scope); stage 3 (observed risk + escalation); stages
4–5 (policy + secret safety); stages 6–7 (build/install + tests w/ baseline-green
& calibrated red→green, repeat/flake/infra-retry policy); stages 8–9 (acceptance
mapping + contract lock w/ locked read-only TestPack); stage 10 (code-quality
delta); stage 11 (RunCheck schema + consistency + untrusted-instruction
rejection); stage 12 (provenance digests + in-toto attestation); stages 13–14
(reviewer aggregation + canary health freshness); GateResult + Slice transition +
stop-the-line.

**Acceptance.** Gate passes only if every required stage passes; each failing
fixture blocks at the expected stage; freshness-key change fails closed with
stale_canary/stale_reviewer_health.

**Refs.** §5, §6.1.1 (provenance), §10 (schema policy), §17, §22.4 (13), §25 P1.11. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED (never-cut).
close_reason: All deterministic gate child beads are implemented and closed: stage framework, stages 1-14, RunCheck, provenance, reviewer/canary freshness, GateResult transitions, and stop-the-line. Latest verification after final stage: full test gate 310 passed, Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.11.1 Gate stage framework + composition + fail-closed
description:
**What.** Gate stage framework + composition + fail-closed.
**Why.** The gate is the heart of the factory; it passes ONLY if every required
stage passes. The stage model must support later epic/phase gates.
**Scope.** stage behaviour (input digests → pass/fail + findings); ordered
composition; required vs advisory; fail-closed default; GateResult assembly hook.
**Acceptance.** Composition runs all stages; any required failure fails the gate.
**Refs.** §17.
notes: Bead-review revision 2026-06-17 (A2): the gate must be invocable in gate-only mode on a given (RunSpec, ContractLock, PatchSet, fixtures) independent of how the patch was produced (real implementer or injected canary mutant), so the gate-canary harness (iqb.12.2) exercises the exact same gate code path. Add to the stage-framework scope + an acceptance criterion.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic gate stage framework with ordered composition, required/advisory semantics, fail-closed exception handling, GateResult attrs assembly, and gate-only facade for injected/canary contexts. Verified with Elixir 1.20 full test gate, Credo, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.11.10 Gate stages 13–14 (reviewer aggregation + canary freshness)
description:
**What.** Stages 13–14: reviewer aggregation + canary health freshness.
**Why.** Every required review exists, evaluated the SAME dossier digest, is
schema-valid, meets the required decision, and comes from a fresh-ReviewerHealth
profile; the latest enabled gate-canary run is green AND fresh for the current
freshness key (else stale_canary, fail closed).
**Scope.** stage 13 (reviewer aggregation + ReviewerHealth freshness); stage 14
(GateHealth freshness key check — gate/policy/test-pack/image/quality/canary-suite/
runcheck-schema digests).
**Acceptance.** Stale reviewer health → stale_reviewer_health; stale canary →
stale_canary; both fail closed.
**Refs.** §17 (13–14), §19.
close_reason: Implemented gate stages 13-14: ReviewerAggregation enforces required review kinds, dossier digest agreement, accepted review decisions, schema field presence, and fresh reviewer health; CanaryFreshness enforces green fresh GateHealth for the current freshness key and false-negative-free canary results. Verified with Elixir 1.20 focused tests, full format/compile/test gate, Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.11.11 GateResult + Slice transition + stop-the-line
description:
**What.** GateResult record + Slice transition + stop-the-line.
**Why.** All required stages must pass; any failure records a GateResult, creates
findings, and moves the Slice to needs_rework/policy_blocked/failed. Stop-the-line
(Phase-1 local): a critical gate/canary failure blocks further runs for the sample
project until addressed.
**Scope.** assemble GateResult (stages, passed, false_negative?, all freshness
digests); transition Slice + RunAttempt; stop-the-line flag.
**Acceptance.** Gate passes only if all required stages pass; failure transitions
correctly and engages stop-the-line.
**Refs.** §17 (stop-the-line), §7.2, §7.3.
close_reason: Implemented GateResult finalization with Slice/RunAttempt transitions and stop-the-line incidents. Verified with focused gate finalizer tests, full test gate (302 passed), Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.11.2 Gate stages 1–2 (workspace integrity + diff scope)
description:
**What.** Stages 1–2: workspace integrity + diff scope.
**Why.** Verify expected base commit, PatchSet applies cleanly to a fresh
checkout, no forbidden/locked files weakened, head tree digest recorded; changed
files + size fit the Slice DiffPolicy.
**Scope.** stage 1 (workspace integrity); stage 2 (diff scope vs DiffPolicy;
unexpected dependency/migration/generated/public-API changes → review or fail per
risk policy).
**Acceptance.** Locked-file weakening fails stage 1; out-of-scope diff fails/
escalates stage 2.
**Refs.** §17 (1–2).
close_reason: Implemented gate stages 1-2: workspace integrity validates base/apply/locked-path/head-tree invariants, and diff scope enforces DiffPolicy path, size, dependency, migration, generated, and public-API bounds. Verified with Elixir 1.20 full test gate, Credo, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.11.3 Gate stage 3 (observed-risk assessment + escalation)
description:
**What.** Stage 3: observed-risk assessment.
**Why.** Actual diff, command behavior, adapter capabilities, dependency changes,
quality deltas, and touched paths are classified via ReviewPolicy.risk_rules. If
observed risk exceeds planned risk, the gate escalates required reviews, requires
human approval, or fails closed.
**Scope.** RiskAssessment computation; escalation logic; required_review_kinds +
required_gate_stages output.
**Acceptance.** Observed > planned risk deterministically escalates or fails
closed.
**Refs.** §6.3 (risk_rules), §17 (3).
close_reason: Implemented gate stage 3 observed-risk assessment: ReviewPolicy risk_rules classify patch facts into observed risk, required review kinds, required gate stages, human approval escalation, and persisted RiskAssessment output. Verified with Elixir 1.20 focused risk-stage tests, full format/compile/test gate, Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.11.4 Gate stages 4–5 (policy + secret safety)
description:
**What.** Stages 4–5: policy + secret safety.
**Why.** No blocked command classes / forbidden env/network / policy-file edits;
no unredacted detected secrets in prompt/logs/diff/manifest/dossier/PR body.
**Scope.** stage 4 (policy: reuse Policy.Engine decisions + ToolInvocation
records); stage 5 (secret safety: consume Security.Redactor findings; blocked
secret prevents pass unless policy allows redacted continuation).
**Acceptance.** A policy-file edit fails stage 4; an unredacted secret fails
stage 5.
**Refs.** §16 (redaction), §17 (4–5).
close_reason: Implemented gate stages 4-5: PolicyCompliance fails closed on protected policy-path edits and blocked/denied ToolInvocation policy records; SecretSafety consumes redactor findings/artifacts/content scans and fails on unredacted or quarantined secrets while allowing redacted continuation when policy permits. Verified with Elixir 1.20 focused tests, full format/compile/test gate, Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.11.5 Gate stages 6–7 (build/install + tests)
description:
**What.** Stages 6–7: build/install + tests.
**Why.** Environment can install/import the app; the conductor reruns required
suites — baseline_regression stays green; locked acceptance suites (previously
calibrated red on base) must pass after the patch. repeat/flake_policy/
infra_retry_policy honored; implementation retries are NOT automatic in Phase 1.
**Scope.** stage 6 (build/install); stage 7 (tests via the rerun engine P1.9.2 +
calibration P1.7.7).
**Acceptance.** Baseline regression green; calibrated acceptance suite goes
red→green; flaky handling fails closed unless quarantined by human decision.
**Refs.** §17 (6–7), §24 (flaky tests).
close_reason: Implemented gate stages 6-7: BuildInstall validates build/install/import command evidence, and TestExecution consumes or invokes VerificationRerunner, enforces green baseline and locked acceptance suites, requires valid base-red acceptance calibration, and fails unapproved flakes. Verified with Elixir 1.20 focused tests, full format/compile/test gate, Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.11.6 Gate stages 8–9 (acceptance mapping + contract lock)
description:
**What.** Stages 8–9: acceptance mapping + contract lock.
**Why.** Every AC has passed evidence (none missing/skipped unless allowed); the
Brief/required tests/AC mapping/verification commands/policy/protected files match
the approved ContractLock; the gate runs the LOCKED TestPack mounted read-only
outside the editable workspace.
**Scope.** stage 8 (acceptance mapping completeness); stage 9 (contract-lock digest
match + locked TestPack read-only execution; repo test changes allowed only if
they don't replace the locked gate tests as acceptance evidence).
**Acceptance.** Missing AC evidence fails stage 8; a weakened/locked-test mismatch
fails stage 9.
**Refs.** §17 (8–9), §22.3.
close_reason: Implemented gate stages 8-9: AcceptanceMapping requires every acceptance criterion to have passed evidence, with explicit handling for missing/skipped/failed results; ContractLock verifies brief, acceptance criteria, required tests, verification commands, policy, TestPack, protected paths, and read-only locked TestPack mount invariants. Verified with Elixir 1.20 focused tests, full format/compile/test gate, Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.11.7 Gate stage 10 (code-quality delta)
description:
**What.** Stage 10: code-quality delta.
**Why.** No new high-risk findings; configured thresholds respected. Quality is a
signal, never sole proof.
**Scope.** compare before/after quality runs; new_high_risk_findings threshold;
gate-blocking only if the adapter declares a deterministic contract + policy
selects it.
**Acceptance.** A new high-risk finding fails the stage when the adapter is
gate-blocking; advisory adapters never block.
**Refs.** §13, §17 (10).
close_reason: Implemented gate stage 10 CodeQualityDelta with deterministic gate-blocking adapter contract checks, configured high-risk threshold enforcement, and advisory-only non-blocking behavior. Verified with focused tests, full test gate (307 passed), Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.11.8 Gate stage 11 (RunCheck schema + consistency)
description:
**What.** Stage 11: RunCheck (schema validity + internal consistency +
untrusted-instruction rejection).
**Why.** Manifest/dossier/evidence/review/gate artifacts must be schema-valid and
internally consistent. RunCheck never best-effort parses unknown versions and must
reject outputs that appear to follow untrusted instructions over the locked
contract.
**Scope.** validate exact schema versions recorded in RunSpec; cross-artifact
consistency (refs/digests/manifest); explicit failures (unsupported_schema_version,
schema_validation_failed); prompt-injection rejection check.
**Acceptance.** Tampered manifest / missing log / mismatched hash / unknown schema
fails; injected-instruction-following output fails.
**Refs.** §5, §10 (schema policy), §17 (11), §18 (artifact_integrity).
close_reason: Implemented gate stage 11 RunCheck: validates required artifacts, exact supported schema versions, artifact/content hashes, RunBundle manifest digest and bundle-root consistency, manifest-to-artifact metadata, and untrusted-instruction-following markers. Verified with Elixir 1.20 focused tests, full format/compile/test gate, Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.11.9 Gate stage 12 (provenance + in-toto attestation)
description:
**What.** Stage 12: provenance + attestation.
**Why.** Required artifacts have content digests; the run records base commit,
patch/prompt/policy/image digests, command invocations, and attestation metadata.
Unsigned local in-toto/SLSA-shaped provenance is acceptable in Phase 1; the
RunBundle root digest is the first trust anchor.
**Scope.** generate provenance.intoto.json (subject digests + materials +
invocation params); optional CycloneDX SBOM; verify all required digests present.
**Acceptance.** Provenance artifact written w/ subject + material digests; missing
required digest fails the stage.
**Refs.** §6.1.1, §17 (12).
close_reason: Implemented gate stage 12 ProvenanceAttestation with local in-toto/SLSA-shaped provenance generation, required subject/material/invocation digest validation, and provenance artifact persistence. Verified with focused tests, full test gate (310 passed), Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.12 — Gate-canary harness + eval suites
description:
# P1.12 — Gate-canary harness + eval suites

**What.** The first eval suite: a labeled mutant set + known-good solution patch
run through the GATE-ONLY path, false-negative/false-positive tracking summarized
into GateHealth, CI failure on any passed mutant, the additional Phase-1 eval
suites, and `mix conveyor.gate_canary`.

**Why / background.** The riskiest assumption in all of Conveyor is that the
verification gate can be trusted — so validate gate honesty IMMEDIATELY, not in a
later phase. A passed mutant is a release-blocking Conveyor bug. A rejected
known-good fixture is also release-blocking if it prevents the tracer bullet from
proving a useful gate.

**Scope.** mutant fixtures + known-good patch (§18 initial mutants); RunGateCanary
worker (gate-only per mutant; assert each fails for the expected/stricter reason;
do NOT invoke the implementer); FN/FP tracking + GateHealth summary + CI fail on
passed mutant; additional eval suites (plan_audit, policy, prompt_injection,
artifact_integrity, adapter_conformance); `mix conveyor.gate_canary`.

**Acceptance.** Every enabled mutant is rejected; false-negative rate reported;
any passed mutant fails CI; known-good fixture passes.

**Refs.** §18, §22.4 (13 canary), §25 P1.12. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED (canaries never-cut).
close_reason: All non-deferred P1.12 child beads are implemented and closed

title: P1.12.1 Canary mutant fixtures + known-good patch
description:
**What.** Canary mutant fixtures + known-good solution patch.
**Why.** Gate canaries are the FIRST eval suite. A known-good patch must pass the
gate-only path; each mutant injects one defect against the known-good solution.
**Scope.** known-good solution patch; the §18 initial mutants
(patch_unknown_id_returns_200, completed_not_persisted_to_list,
default_completed_missing, test_weakened_or_deleted, new_codescent_high_risk,
forbidden_policy_edit, repo_prompt_injection_ignored, tool_output_injection_ignored)
+ expected catch each; mutants.json fixture.
**Acceptance.** Fixtures exist with labeled expected reasons.
**Refs.** §18.
close_reason: Added Phase-1 gate canary fixture manifest, known-good patch, eight initial mutant patches with labeled expected catches, and tests validating manifest shape plus git-apply patch validity. Verified with focused fixture tests, full test gate (314 passed), Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.12.2 RunGateCanary worker (gate-only mutant checks)
description:
**What.** RunGateCanary worker (gate-only path).
**Why.** Run the known-good patch and each mutant through the GATE ONLY (do not
invoke the implementer); assert the known-good passes and every mutant fails for
the expected or an equally-valid stricter reason.
**Scope.** Oban worker iterating fixtures through the gate; record CanaryRun
artifacts; map failures to expected stage/reason.
**Acceptance.** Known-good passes; every enabled mutant is rejected at the
expected stage.
**Refs.** §18, §22.4 (13 canary).
notes: Bead-review revision 2026-06-17 (A2): RunGateCanary invokes the gate via the gate-only entry point from iqb.11.1 (same code path), passing each mutant as a synthetic PatchSet/RunSpec with no implementer session.
close_reason: Implemented RunGateCanary worker using RunGate.run_gate_only!/3, synthetic canary PatchSet context, expected catch classification, false-negative/false-positive summary, and optional canary run artifact persistence. Verified with focused worker tests, full test gate (317 passed), Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.12.3 FN/FP tracking + GateHealth summary + CI fail
description:
**What.** False-negative/false-positive tracking + GateHealth summary + CI fail.
**Why.** A passed mutant is a release-blocking Conveyor bug; a rejected known-good
fixture is also release-blocking. GateHealth is the single queryable summary the
gate consults for freshness/honesty.
**Scope.** track FN (mutants wrongly passed) + FP (good fixtures wrongly rejected);
summarize into GateHealth (keyed by freshness key); fail CI on any FN.
**Acceptance.** FN rate reported; any passed mutant fails CI; GateHealth row
updated with the freshness key.
**Refs.** §17 (freshness), §18.
close_reason: Implemented canary false-negative/false-positive summary tracking, CI exit code 6 for FN/FP failures, and GateHealth upsert keyed by CanaryFreshness freshness inputs. Verified with focused canary tests, full test gate (318 passed), Credo strict, and Dialyzer.

title: P1.12.4 Additional eval suites (prompt_injection, artifact_integrity, ...)
description:
**What.** Additional Phase-1 eval suites.
**Why.** Gate canaries are the first eval suite, not the only one. These labeled
suites cover the other threat classes.
**Scope.** plan_audit (reuse P0.4.7), policy (reuse P0.6.8), prompt_injection
(repo/tool-output instructions must not override policy/locked Brief),
artifact_integrity (tampered manifests/missing logs/mismatched hashes/stale canary
refs fail RunCheck), adapter_conformance (reuse P1.8.6); stable pass/fail report.
**Acceptance.** Each suite produces a stable pass/fail report; prompt-injection +
artifact-integrity suites pass.
**Refs.** §18 (additional suites), §23 (eval harness tests).
close_reason: Implemented Conveyor.EvalSuites stable Phase-1 eval report in lib/conveyor/eval_suites.ex with manifest test/fixtures/eval_suites/phase1.json and tests in test/conveyor/eval_suites_test.exs. Verified PGPORT=55432 mix test test/conveyor/eval_suites_test.exs test/conveyor/plan_audit_eval_test.exs test/conveyor/policy/eval_fixtures_test.exs test/conveyor/gate_stage_run_check_test.exs test/conveyor/gate_canary_fixtures_test.exs test/conveyor/run_gate_canary_test.exs test/conveyor/code_quality_adapter_test.exs test/conveyor/test_result_adapter_test.exs (36 passed).

title: P1.12.5 mix conveyor.gate_canary CLI
description:
**What.** `mix conveyor.gate_canary PROJECT_ID`.
**Why.** Operator surface for the canary harness; emits machine-readable JSON +
the §9 exit code (6 on false negative).
**Scope.** CLI running the canary worker; print summary + write canary/mutants.json;
exit code 6 on FN.
**Acceptance.** Runs the enabled mutant set; exits 6 if any mutant passes.
**Refs.** §9, §18.
close_reason: Implemented mix conveyor.gate_canary PROJECT_ID in lib/mix/tasks/conveyor.gate_canary.ex. The task invokes Conveyor.Jobs.RunGateCanary, prints JSON, writes canary/mutants.json by default, and exits with the runner ci_exit_code (6 on false negatives). Verified PGPORT=55432 mix test test/mix/tasks/conveyor_gate_canary_test.exs test/conveyor/run_gate_canary_test.exs test/conveyor/gate_canary_fixtures_test.exs (9 passed) and mix format --check-formatted on touched files.

title: P1.12.6 Design-law invariant test suite (laws 1-10)
description:
**What.** An executable test suite asserting the 10 design laws (§3) as INVARIANTS, not aspirational prose.
**Why.** §3 states the design laws "should be tested as invariants, not treated as aspirational prose." Today each law is enforced emergently across feature beads; no single suite asserts them, risking silent erosion.
**Scope.** at least one test per law — e.g. law 1 (no task without ACs → readiness blocks); law 2 (implementer cannot weaken a locked test/contract); law 3 (agent self-report alone never marks done — independent verification required); law 5 (every material transition appends exactly one LedgerEvent); law 6 (one isolated container per run); law 7 (context tools never mutate source); law 8 (no dangerous command by default); law 9 (orphan requirement/Slice blocks handoff). Cross-reference the feature beads enforcing each law.
**Acceptance.** Each design law has at least one executable assertion; violating a law fails CI.
**Refs.** §3, §23. Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED.
[Bead-review addition 2026-06-17 — finding C1.]
close_reason: Implemented in lib/conveyor/design_laws.ex and test/conveyor/design_laws_invariant_test.exs

title: P1.13 — Static report + minimal LiveView complete
description:
# P1.13 — Static report + minimal LiveView complete

**What.** Write the full artifact set (manifest/dossier/evidence/review/gate/
diff/pr_body) and PR-body draft, and complete the LiveView to render those same
records plus live station status, gate stages, canary, incidents, reviewer,
quality delta, export controls, and the human-approval / mark-externally-merged
control.

**Why / background.** Phase 1 is headless-first: the dossier must be usable
WITHOUT LiveView and CI must produce all artifacts headlessly. The PR-body draft
is generated even though Phase 1 merge is manual — this forces evidence quality
to match the later L2 promise.

**Scope.** full artifact write (§16); PR-body draft generator (§16.3); LiveView
complete (§21 list); human approval / "mark externally merged" control requiring
either an external commit hash or explicit not_integrated decision.

**Acceptance.** Dossier usable without LiveView; CI produces all artifacts
headlessly; LiveView renders the same records plus live station status.

**Refs.** §16, §16.2, §16.3, §21, §22.4 (14), §25 P1.13. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED (report; LiveView polish cut-first).
close_reason: All P1.13 child beads are implemented and closed

title: P1.13.1 Full artifact set write
description:
**What.** Full artifact set write (manifest/dossier/evidence/review/gate/diff/
pr_body).
**Why.** Headless-first: the dossier must be usable WITHOUT LiveView and CI must
produce all artifacts headlessly.
**Scope.** ensure the projector writes the complete §16 set for a run attempt;
machine + human artifacts consistent.
**Acceptance.** All artifacts present + schema-valid for a completed run.
**Refs.** §16, §22.4 (14).
close_reason: Implemented full headless artifact set projection in lib/conveyor/artifacts/projector/local_disk.ex: manifest.json plus dossier.md, evidence.json, review.json, gate.json, diff.patch, and pr_body.md. Added schema-valid projection coverage in test/conveyor/artifacts/projector_test.exs and updated report task expectations. Verified PGPORT=55432 mix test test/conveyor/artifacts/projector_test.exs test/conveyor/replay_test.exs test/mix/tasks/conveyor_report_test.exs test/conveyor/gate_stage_run_check_test.exs (17 passed) and mix format --check-formatted on touched files.

title: P1.13.2 PR-body draft generator
description:
**What.** PR-body draft generator (pr_body.md).
**Why.** Phase-1 merge is manual and no PR is opened, but generating the PR body
forces evidence quality to match the later L2 promise.
**Scope.** §16.3 template (Task, Summary, Acceptance checkboxes, Verification,
Risk, Agent, Evidence digests: run_bundle/dossier/gate).
**Acceptance.** pr_body.md generated with verification checklist + evidence
digests.
**Refs.** §16.3.
close_reason: Implemented PR-body draft generation as part of artifact projection in lib/conveyor/artifacts/projector/local_disk.ex. pr_body.md now includes the §16.3 sections, verification checklist, and run_bundle/dossier/gate digests without making the PR body part of the bundle hash. Verified PGPORT=55432 mix test test/conveyor/artifacts/projector_test.exs test/conveyor/replay_test.exs test/mix/tasks/conveyor_report_test.exs test/conveyor/gate_stage_run_check_test.exs (18 passed) and mix format --check-formatted on touched files.

title: P1.13.3 LiveView complete (gate/canary/reviewer/incidents/export)
description:
**What.** Complete the minimal LiveView (render full run records + live status).
**Why.** LiveView is a live projection of the same records; keep it minimal
(polish is cut-first).
**Scope.** §21 panels: plan audit score/findings; Slice state + ledger timeline;
live agent events/heartbeat; ContextPack; RunPrompt version/policy; evidence
AC→proof; CodeScent before/after; reviewer verdict; gate stages; canary status;
incidents; export controls.
**Acceptance.** LiveView renders the same records the static report shows, plus
live station status.
**Refs.** §21, §22.4 (14).
close_reason: Implemented full Run Viewer projection in lib/conveyor_web/live/run_viewer_live.ex with coverage in test/conveyor_web/live/run_viewer_live_test.exs

title: P1.13.4 Human approval / mark-externally-merged control
description:
**What.** Human approval / "mark externally merged" control.
**Why.** Phase-1 integration is a manual human action; Conveyor records the
decision and requires either an external commit hash or an explicit
not_integrated decision.
**Scope.** LiveView + CLI control creating a HumanApproval; capture external_commit
or not_integrated; trigger post-integration check (P1.14.4).
**Acceptance.** Control records a HumanApproval requiring a commit hash or an
explicit not_integrated decision.
**Refs.** §21, §22.4 (15).
close_reason: Implemented HumanIntegration.record!/1, mix conveyor.mark_externally_merged, and RunViewer LiveView human approval controls. Decisions now require either --external-commit or --not-integrated, and HumanApproval supports :not_integrated with a DB constraint migration. Verified PGPORT=55432 mix test test/conveyor/human_integration_test.exs test/mix/tasks/conveyor_mark_externally_merged_test.exs test/conveyor_web/live/run_viewer_live_test.exs test/conveyor/factory/safety_audit_resources_test.exs (12 passed) and mix format --check-formatted on touched files.

title: P1.14 — End-to-end tracer run + orchestration
description:
# P1.14 — End-to-end tracer run + orchestration

**What.** The `RunSlice` station orchestrator (+ StationRun leases/idempotency,
StationEffect reconciliation, SandboxReaper), the operator commands
(demo/run_slice/ci/verify/contract_diff), the post-integration check + patch
equivalence, and the full hermetic E2E test driving one approved Slice to gated →
manual done with R0/R1 replay. Live Pi behind a tagged test.

**Why / background.** This is where the loop becomes real. A single `RunSlice`
job advances a Slice station by station, but each long-running station is an Oban
job with idempotent inputs/outputs giving crash/reboot recovery. After crashes,
unknown StationEffects must be reconciled against the real world before resume.
The human records external integration manually; Conveyor computes patch
equivalence conservatively and reruns post-integration checks.

**Scope.** RunSlice orchestrator + StationRun lease/idempotency-key +
StationEffect declare/reconcile + ReconcileStaleEffects + SandboxReaper;
`mix conveyor.demo` (hermetic; fake runner; no provider; no network); run_slice/
ci/verify wiring; contract_diff (§9.6) + contract-evolution rule; post-integration
check + PatchEquivalence + ExternalChange; full E2E test (approved→gated→manual
done) + R0/R1 replay; live Pi @tag :live_agent.

**Acceptance.** One human action drives an approved Slice to gated; human merges
manually to done; run replays from event log and artifacts regenerate; demo runs
in CI with no credential.

**Refs.** §8 (topology + retry rules), §9.4, §9.5, §9.6, §21 (equivalence),
§22.4 (1,15,16), §23, §25 P1.14. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: Completed P1.14 tracer orchestration: RunSlice, demo/operator CLI, effect reconciliation, contract_diff, post-integration checks, LiveView/reporting, and E2E replay coverage

title: P1.14.1 RunSlice orchestrator + station leases/idempotency (happy-path)
description:
**What.** RunSlice station orchestrator + StationRun leases/idempotency +
StationEffect declare/reconcile + ReconcileStaleEffects + SandboxReaper.
**Why.** A single RunSlice job advances a Slice station by station; each
long-running station is an Oban job with idempotent inputs/outputs giving
crash/reboot recovery. Unknown StationEffects must be reconciled against the real
world before resume; periodic reconciliation inspects sandbox/process/lease/
artifact state, never assuming the DB is the whole truth.
**Scope.** RunSlice orchestrator threading the StationPlan DAG; StationRun
creation/resume by idempotency key (run_attempt_id+station_key+spec_sha256+
attempt_no); lease acquire/refresh + heartbeat; StationEffect declare-before-exec +
reconcile; the 8-rule station job contract (§8); ReconcileStaleEffects +
SandboxReaper workers.
**Acceptance.** Killing a station mid-run and resuming does not duplicate effects/
artifacts/ledger events; stale leases + unknown effects are reconciled.
**Refs.** §8, §8.1 (publication), §23 (idempotency tests).
notes: Bead-review revision 2026-06-17 (D1/B2/A1): scope narrowed to the RunSlice orchestrator -- threads the StationPlan over the station behaviour (q19.6.8), creates/resumes StationRuns by idempotency key, lease acquire/refresh + heartbeat, happy-path station advancement, outbox publication. Moved out: reusable station execution contract -> q19.6.8; StationEffect reconcile + ReconcileStaleEffects -> iqb.14.7; SandboxReaper owned by iqb.7.3 (not rebuilt here). Estimate reduced 600 -> 360.
close_reason: Implemented RunSlice orchestrator in lib/conveyor/run_slice.ex and RunSlice worker delegation in lib/conveyor/jobs/run_slice.ex

title: P1.14.2 mix conveyor.demo (hermetic tracer)
description:
**What.** `mix conveyor.demo` hermetic tracer.
**Why.** The default OSS onboarding + CI smoke test + regression test for every
future refactor: deterministic patch runner, deterministic reviewer fixture,
LocalPython/Noop quality adapter, no live provider credential, no CodeScent
requirement, no network egress.
**Scope.** wire the full station flow with the Fake runner + reviewer fixture;
write the artifact projection; print a short summary.
**Acceptance.** Runs the full Phase-1 loop in hermetic CI with no credential and
no network.
**Refs.** §9.5, §22, §23.
close_reason: Implemented hermetic mix conveyor.demo in lib/conveyor/demo.ex and lib/mix/tasks/conveyor.demo.ex

title: P1.14.3 run_slice / ci / verify command wiring
description:
**What.** Wire `mix conveyor.run_slice` / `mix conveyor.ci` / `mix conveyor.verify`.
**Why.** Operator surfaces for running a real Slice, headless CI mode, and
re-verifying a run attempt.
**Scope.** run_slice (drive one Slice); ci (headless; machine JSON + stable exit
code); verify (independent re-verification of a RUN_ATTEMPT_ID); show (§9).
**Acceptance.** Each command runs and emits the documented output + exit code.
**Refs.** §9, §9.4.
notes: Bead-review revision 2026-06-17 (C3): add mix conveyor.show SLICE_ID (section 9) as an explicit deliverable with its own acceptance criterion (currently only implied in scope).
close_reason: Implemented mix conveyor.run_slice, conveyor.ci, conveyor.verify, and conveyor.show

title: P1.14.4 Post-integration check + PatchEquivalence + ExternalChange
description:
**What.** Post-integration check + PatchEquivalence + ExternalChange.
**Why.** Station 16. The human integrates externally; Conveyor computes patch
equivalence conservatively and reruns required verification at the external commit
before allowing done.
**Scope.** reconstruct accepted patch from blobs; diff base→external commit;
normalized patch identity; equivalence (exact / equivalent_with_human_edits /
divergent / partial); protected-path checks; rerun post_integration suites at the
external commit; block done on unexpected divergence.
**Acceptance.** exact/equivalent records done-eligible; divergent/partial blocks
done; post-integration suites rerun at the external commit.
**Refs.** §21 (equivalence algorithm), §22.4 (16).
close_reason: Implemented PostIntegration patch equivalence and ExternalChange/PatchEquivalence recording

title: P1.14.5 mix conveyor.contract_diff + contract-evolution rule
description:
**What.** `mix conveyor.contract_diff` + contract-evolution rule.
**Why.** Before a rerun, render a contract diff so it's clear whether a second
attempt retries the same contract or a changed one. acceptance_weakened /
policy_weakened block automatic rerun and require a human approval reason; any
contract-affecting change creates a new ContractLock + RunSpec + RunAttempt and
requires a HumanDecision.
**Scope.** classify changes (clarification_only/scope_added/scope_removed/
acceptance_weakened/strengthened/policy_weakened/strengthened/test_pack_changed);
enforce the contract-evolution rule (§6.0).
**Acceptance.** Weakening changes block automatic rerun; a contract change forces
new lock/spec/attempt + HumanDecision.
**Refs.** §6.0, §9.6.
close_reason: Implemented contract diff classification, contract-evolution rerun state creation, HumanDecision audit, and mix conveyor.contract_diff with tests

title: P1.14.6 Full E2E tracer test + R0/R1 replay + live Pi (@tag :live_agent)
description:
**What.** Full end-to-end tracer test + R0/R1 replay + live Pi (tagged).
**Why.** The Phase-1 definition of done: one human action drives an approved Slice
to gated; the human merges manually to done; the run replays from the event log
and artifacts regenerate. The live Pi path runs behind @tag :live_agent.
**Scope.** E2E test (seed→audit→readiness→baseline→calibration→scout→prompt→
fake-implement→evidence→review→gate→canary→report→approve→post-integration→done);
assert R0/R1 replay; @tag :live_agent test exercising the same flow with real Pi.
**Acceptance.** E2E passes hermetically; replay regenerates timeline + artifacts;
live Pi flow passes behind the tag.
**Refs.** §22.4, §23, §25 P1.14.
close_reason: Added hermetic E2E tracer/replay test and demo lifecycle completion to reported

title: P1.14.7 StationEffect declare/reconcile + ReconcileStaleEffects worker
description:
**What.** StationEffect declare-before-exec + crash reconciliation, and the periodic ReconcileStaleEffects worker — split out of the former monolithic iqb.14.1 so happy-path orchestration and crash-recovery are separately testable.
**Why.** §8: after crashes/node restarts/cancelled jobs/daemon restarts, unknown StationEffects must be reconciled against the REAL world (sandbox/process/lease/artifact state) before resume — reconciliation never assumes the DB is the whole truth. This recovery concern is distinct from advancing stations on the happy path (iqb.14.1).
**Scope.** StationEffect declare-before-exec lifecycle; the station retry rule (an unknown effect must be reconciled first); ReconcileStaleEffects periodic worker (expired lease / missing heartbeat / unknown effect → inspect runtime, mark reconciled/failed, then resume/retry/cancel/fail); integrates with the SandboxReaper owned by P1.7.3 (does NOT rebuild it).
**Acceptance.** killing a station mid-run leaves no orphaned container/effect after reconciliation; an unknown effect is reconciled before any resume.
**Refs.** §8. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
[Bead-review addition 2026-06-17 — finding D1: split from iqb.14.1.]
close_reason: Implemented StationEffect stale reconciliation, retry blocking for unknown effects, and ReconcileStaleEffects worker with tests

title: P1.15 — Retrospective record + swarm-readiness instrumentation
description:
# P1.15 — Retrospective record + swarm-readiness instrumentation

**What.** Capture the Phase-1 retrospective (timings, cost/tokens if available,
adapter friction, failure taxonomy, gate-canary stats, schema friction), audit
that all §27 swarm-readiness fields are recorded, and answer the §28 success
questionnaire with evidence.

**Why / background.** Phase 1 is single-run, but each run should record the
future scheduling/evaluation data (likely_files, conflict_domains, station
durations, FN rate, time-to-first-diff, etc.). Capturing them now costs little;
inventing them after dozens of runs would lose the eval dataset. The
retrospective is what tells us the minimum next step: better plan compiler, gate,
adapter, or parallelism.

**Scope.** retrospective capture (§20 failure taxonomy + timings); swarm-readiness
field audit (§27 — verify every field is captured); §28 success questionnaire
answered with evidence.

**Acceptance.** Report states whether Phase 2/3 assumptions still hold and which
next investment is justified by evidence.

**Refs.** §20, §27, §28, §25 P1.15. Cutline: INSTRUMENT_ONLY.
close_reason: Completed retrospective capture, §27 swarm-readiness audit, §28 success report, and §12 threat-matrix audit instrumentation

title: P1.15.1 Retrospective capture (failure taxonomy + timings)
description:
**What.** Retrospective capture.
**Why.** Every failed/successful run should teach which station needs improvement.
"Agent failed" is too vague — record the failure taxonomy + timings.
**Scope.** capture timings, token/cost estimate (if available), adapter friction,
failure taxonomy (§20 categories), gate-canary stats, schema friction; rework
handoff template.
**Acceptance.** A run produces a structured retrospective record.
**Refs.** §20, §25 P1.15.
close_reason: Added structured retrospective record generation and projected retrospective.json with failure taxonomy, timings, adapter friction, canary stats, schema friction, and rework handoff coverage

title: P1.15.2 Swarm-readiness field instrumentation audit (§27)
description:
**What.** Swarm-readiness field instrumentation audit.
**Why.** Capturing the §27 fields now costs little; inventing them after dozens of
runs would lose the eval dataset for the future scheduler/conflict-heatmap/agent-
reputation/economic-governor.
**Scope.** verify every §27 field is recorded (likely_files, conflict_domains,
station durations/retries, heartbeat gaps, queue latency, FN rate, time-to-first-
diff, time-to-green, image pull/create time, cache hit/miss, gate workspace
materialization time, rework category, cost/tokens, files/lines changed, review +
human merge decisions, post-merge notes).
**Acceptance.** An audit confirms all §27 fields are captured by a real run.
**Refs.** §27.
close_reason: Added executable §27 swarm-readiness audit, exposed it in retrospective.json, and preserved deterministic run-bundle replay with Phase-1 placeholders for post-projection fields

title: P1.15.3 §28 success questionnaire answered with evidence
description:
**What.** Answer the §28 Phase-1 success questionnaire with evidence.
**Why.** Phase 1 is successful only if it answers these questions with evidence;
the answers decide the minimum next step (better plan compiler / gate / adapter /
parallelism).
**Scope.** answer each §28 question (loop feels right? audit distinguishes
executable handoff? gate zero FN on initial mutants? Pi/RPC seam clean? Ash
schemas stable? AGENTS.md reduces ambiguity? CodeScent useful-not-proof? artifacts
PR-ready? policy effective?) citing run evidence; state whether Phase 2/3
assumptions hold.
**Acceptance.** A written report answers all §28 questions with cited evidence +
a recommended next investment.
**Refs.** §28.
close_reason: Added docs/2_implementation_plans/PHASE-1-SUCCESS-QUESTIONNAIRE.md answering every §28 question with evidence and recommending gate hardening before parallelism

title: P1.15.4 Threat-matrix completeness audit (12.0)
description:
**What.** An audit verifying every threat class in the §12.0 threat model has at least one Phase-1 test, canary, or doctor check (as §12.0 requires).
**Why.** §12.0: "Each threat class must have at least one Phase-1 test, canary, or doctor check." Defenses exist per row, but no bead verifies all 11 threats are actually covered — a gap could go unnoticed.
**Scope.** enumerate the 11 §12.0 threats (malicious repo content, malicious tool output, agent policy evasion, test weakening, secret exposure, supply-chain drift, artifact tampering, reviewer rubber-stamp, gate false negative, internal state corruption, host escape); map each to its enforcing test/canary/doctor check (policy evals, prompt-injection suite, artifact-integrity suite, redaction tests, canary mutants, network-segregation test, sandbox hardening, etc.); fail if any threat is unmapped. Sibling to the §27 swarm-field audit (P1.15.2).
**Acceptance.** the audit maps all 11 threats to at least one check each and fails on any gap.
**Refs.** §12.0, §18, §23. Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED / INSTRUMENT_ONLY.
[Bead-review addition 2026-06-17 — finding C2.]
close_reason: Added executable §12 threat-matrix audit covering all 11 threat classes with existing test/canary/doctor references and documentation

title: P1.2 — Human-authored plan, Brief, locked tests, seed
description:
# P1.2 — Human-authored plan, Brief, locked tests, seed

**What.** Author the Phase-1 plan (markdown narrative + `conveyor.plan.yml`),
human-authored failing pytest acceptance cases, the first Slice AgentBrief with
ContractLock and a locked read-only TestPack, and `mix conveyor.seed_sample`.

**Why / background.** Design laws 1–2: no task without acceptance criteria; no
implementation without a locked contract. The human acts as Test Architect and
commits VISIBLE failing tests before the implementer runs. The implementer may
SEE the tests, but the gate executes the LOCKED pack from a read-only mount
outside the editable workspace, so tests cannot be weakened to fake success.

**Scope.** plan.md + conveyor.plan.yml (§22.2); locked acceptance tests
(AC-001..005, §22.3); AgentBrief + ContractLock + TestPack (locked_at/by, digests);
`mix conveyor.seed_sample` (Project→Plan→Requirement→Epic→Slice→Brief + base commit).

**Acceptance.** New tests fail before implementation; existing tests pass; seed
creates the full graph and records the base commit.

**Refs.** §6.0 (contract evolution), §22.2, §22.3, §25 P1.2. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED (locked TestPack never-cut).
close_reason: Completed P1.2 child beads: plan.md + conveyor.plan.yml validated (iqb.2.1), RED pytest acceptance cases committed with expected 4 failing acceptance tests and 3 passing baseline tests (iqb.2.2), first-slice AgentBrief/ContractLock/locked TestPack created with digests and protected paths (iqb.2.3), and mix conveyor.seed_sample implemented idempotently with base commit recording (iqb.2.4). Latest Elixir 1.20 repo gate for the seed work: format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, full mix test (164 passed), credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P1.2.1 Phase-1 plan.md + conveyor.plan.yml
description:

non_goals, requirements, acceptance_criteria w/ required_test_refs,
verification_commands, decisions, slices w/ likely_files/conflict_domains/
autonomy_ceiling L1).

**Refs.** §10, §22.2.

Added PlanContract coverage for the sample sidecar. Verified with Elixir 1.20: format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, 160 tests, credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P1.2.2 Locked acceptance pytest cases (red before impl)
description:

title: P1.2.3 AgentBrief + ContractLock + locked TestPack
description:
**What.** First Slice AgentBrief + ContractLock + locked read-only TestPack.
**Why.** No implementation without a locked contract. The implementer may SEE the
tests, but the gate executes the LOCKED pack from a read-only mount outside the
editable workspace; repository test files may be improved but cannot replace or
weaken the locked acceptance evidence.
**Scope.** AgentBrief (current/desired behavior, key interfaces, ACs, required
tests, verification commands, out_of_scope, locked_at/by); ContractLock (all
*_sha256 digests + protected_path_globs); TestPack (test_pack_ref/sha256,
runner_command_specs, mount_path, test_result_adapter, locked).
**Acceptance.** Brief + lock + TestPack created with digests; protected paths set.
**Refs.** §6.0, §6.1, §22.3.

Added a locked copy of the sample acceptance tests under samples/tasks_service/.conveyor/test-packs/tasks-complete/v1 and DB coverage proving AgentBrief, TestPack, and ContractLock resources can be created with matching digests. Verified with Elixir 1.20: focused sample_tasks_contract_test (2 passed), format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, full mix test (162 passed), credo --strict, and dialyzer.

description:

**Why.** Station 3 of the tracer (§22.4): create the full work graph and record

The seed creates the sample Project, Plan, Requirements, HumanDecisions, Epic, Slice, AgentBrief, TestPack, ContractLock, and initial RunSpec with the first recorded base commit, and re-seed preserves existing immutable rows and the original base commit without duplicates. title: P1.3 — Plan audit gate (run on sample)
description:
# P1.3 — Plan audit gate (run on sample)

**What.** Exercise `mix conveyor.plan_audit` against the seeded sample plan and
the deliberately-broken fixtures.

**Why / background.** Proves the P0.4 audit machinery on the real Phase-1 plan —
the first station of the loop (§22.4 step 4). Treating manual input as
automatically executable is the bad habit this gate prevents.

**Acceptance.** Sample plan reaches handoff_ready; missing-AC / missing-test /
missing-decision fixtures fail with stable findings.

**Refs.** §22.4 (4), §25 P1.3. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: Completed P1.3 by exercising mix conveyor.plan_audit on the seeded sample plan and deliberately broken missing-AC, missing-test, and missing-decision fixtures. Sample reaches handoff_ready; broken fixtures block with stable findings. Latest verification for child iqb.3.1: focused plan audit tests (12 passed), full mix test (167 passed), credo --strict, and dialyzer under Elixir 1.20.

title: P1.3.1 Run plan_audit on sample + failing fixtures
description:

**Why.** Proves the P0.4 audit on the real Phase-1 plan (station 4) and that
broken handoffs are blocked.
**Scope.** run audit on sample → handoff_ready; missing-AC / missing-test /
missing-decision fixtures → blocked; assert findings.
**Acceptance.** Sample reaches handoff_ready; each broken fixture fails with

**Refs.** §22.4 (4), §25 P1.3.
close_reason: Exercised mix conveyor.plan_audit against the seeded sample plan and broken fixtures. Plan audit now reuses seeded Project/Plan records by local path and contract digest, the sample sidecar reaches handoff_ready, missing-ac/missing-test/missing-decision fixtures block with stable findings, and the missing-decision fixture is snapshotted. Tightened sample DEC-001 wording to avoid vague audit language. Verified with Elixir 1.20: focused plan audit tests (12 passed), format --check-formatted, compile --warnings-as-errors, full mix test (167 passed), credo --strict, and dialyzer.

title: P1.4 — Readiness gate
description:
# P1.4 — Readiness gate

**What.** `Readiness.check/1` confirms a locked Brief with concrete acceptance
criteria, required tests, key interfaces, out-of-scope, risk, and matching
ContractLock content hashes before a Slice may run.

**Why / background.** Readiness is the gate between "approved" and "ready" — the
second contract gate after plan audit (§22.4 step 5). It catches vague/oversized
Briefs before any container spins up.

**Scope.** Readiness.check/1; failure modes (vague/testless →
needs_clarification/too_large).

**Acceptance.** Complete Brief → ready; vague or testless Brief →
needs_clarification/too_large.

**Refs.** §22.4 (5), §25 P1.4. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: Completed P1.4 readiness gate: Readiness.check/1 locked Brief validation (099a163) and failure-mode classification/tests for needs_clarification and too_large (61c14f7). Latest full Elixir 1.20 gate passed with 184 tests, credo --strict clean, dialyzer passed with existing ignored warning count.

title: P1.4.1 Readiness.check/1 (locked Brief validation)
description:
**What.** `Readiness.check/1`.
**Why.** Station 5: confirm a locked Brief with concrete ACs, required tests, key
interfaces, out-of-scope, risk, and matching ContractLock content hashes before a
container spins up.
**Scope.** validate Brief lock + ContractLock digests match; concrete ACs;
required tests present; → Slice ready.
**Acceptance.** Complete Brief → ready.
**Refs.** §22.4 (5), §25 P1.4.
close_reason: Implemented Conveyor.Readiness.check/1 for locked Brief validation. It verifies handoff-ready Plan, latest AgentBrief, ContractLock, TestPack, required Brief fields, acceptance criteria/tests, and ContractLock digests; passing checks transition the Slice to ready. Verified with Elixir 1.20: focused readiness tests (2 passed), full gate (180 passed), credo --strict clean, dialyzer passed with existing ignored warning count.

title: P1.4.2 Readiness failure modes + tests
description:

too_large before implementation.
**Scope.** detect missing ACs / no required tests / vague wording; map to
needs_clarification/too_large; tests for each.
**Acceptance.** Vague/testless Brief → needs_clarification/too_large.
**Refs.** §25 P1.4, §20 (Brief Failure).

Vague/testless/missing briefs return needs_clarification; oversized briefs return too_large; lock mismatches remain blocked. Verified with Elixir 1.20: focused readiness tests (6 passed), full gate (184 passed), credo --strict clean, dialyzer passed with existing ignored warning count.

title: P1.5 — Context Scout + quality-signal baseline
description:
# P1.5 — Context Scout + quality-signal baseline

**What.** The read-only `ContextScout` station producing a cited `ContextPack`,
plus the `CodeQualityAdapter` family (Noop + LocalPython defaults; optional
CodeScent) and the before-work quality baseline artifact.

**Why / background.** The scout reduces agent confusion before the implementer
gets edit authority. Design law 7: context tools never mutate source. Quality
signals are risk/context/gate-delta signals — NEVER sole proof. For an
OSS-friendly default, the demo must work with the Noop/local adapter; CodeScent
is an optional advanced adapter.

**Scope.** ContextScout (rg + file tree + Plan/Req/Decision/Slice/Brief +
AGENTS.md) → ContextPack (relevant_files w/ reasons, key_interfaces, existing
tests, risks, suggested validation, confidence); CodeQualityAdapter behaviour +
Noop + LocalPython; optional CodeScent adapter + baseline artifact + adapter
conformance contract.

**Acceptance.** Pack names router/model/tests with reasons and confidence;
baseline quality artifact recorded; Noop/local path needs no proprietary tools.

**Refs.** §13, §22.4 (8), §25 P1.5. Cutline: TRUST_REQUIRED (advanced CodeScent cut-first).
notes: Bead-review revision 2026-06-17 (B1): priority raised to P1 (was P2). The minimal ContextScout station + Noop/LocalPython quality adapter (iqb.5.1 and iqb.5.2, also raised to P1) are on the P1 tracer critical path -- they block the P1 prompt builder iqb.6. Cutline clarification: the minimal scout is TRACER_REQUIRED; only the advanced CodeScent adapter (iqb.5.3, stays P3) is cut-first. Also now blocked by the station-execution behaviour q19.6.8 (finding A1).
close_reason: ContextScout, Noop/LocalPython quality adapters, and optional CodeScent adapter are implemented, verified, committed, and pushed.

title: P1.5.1 ContextScout station → ContextPack
description:
**What.** ContextScout station → cited ContextPack.
**Why.** Read-only station (design law 7: no source mutation) that reduces agent
confusion before the implementer gets edit authority.
**Scope.** inputs (Plan/Req/Decision/Slice/Brief + AGENTS.md + config + rg/file
tree + quality signals + existing tests); output ContextPack (relevant_files w/
reasons, key_interfaces, existing_tests, risks, suggested_validation, confidence,
code_quality_refs).
**Acceptance.** Pack names router/model/tests with reasons + confidence.
**Refs.** §13, §22.4 (8).
close_reason: Implemented ContextScout station in lib/conveyor/context_scout.ex with persisted ContextPack output and coverage in test/conveyor/context_scout_test.exs.

title: P1.5.2 CodeQualityAdapter behaviour + Noop + LocalPython
description:
**What.** CodeQualityAdapter behaviour + Noop + LocalPython adapters.
**Why.** For an OSS-friendly default, the demo must work WITHOUT proprietary
tools. Quality signals are risk/context/gate-delta — never sole proof.
**Scope.** CodeQualityAdapter/ScoutSignalAdapter behaviour (before/after/gate);
Noop (advisory only); LocalPython (configured local tools); result schema
conveyor.quality_result@1.
**Acceptance.** Noop + LocalPython produce valid results; default demo needs no
proprietary tools.
**Refs.** §13.
close_reason: Implemented CodeQualityAdapter behavior, schema result, Noop and LocalPython adapters, persisted CodeQualityRun output, and focused adapter coverage.

title: P1.5.3 Optional CodeScent adapter + baseline + conformance
description:
**What.** Optional CodeScent adapter + baseline artifact + conformance contract.
**Why.** CodeScent is an optional ADVANCED adapter (cut-first); it may block the
gate only if it declares a deterministic adapter_contract (version command,
result schema, fixture suite, threshold policy).
**Scope.** CodeScent adapter; before-work baseline artifact (codescent/before.json);
adapter_contract + fixture conformance; gate-blocking only when policy selects it.
**Acceptance.** Baseline artifact recorded; adapter passes conformance; absent
CodeScent never breaks the default demo.
**Refs.** §13, §22.4 (8).
close_reason: Implemented optional CodeScent adapter contract, before-work baseline helper, conformance fixture test, and missing-tool blocked result; default adapters remain independent.

title: P1.6 — Prompt builder
description:
# P1.6 — Prompt builder

**What.** `PromptBuilder` emits a versioned, immutable prompt envelope (§14) from
structured inputs (Brief, ContextPack, AGENTS.md, policy, required tests, output
schema), with explicit trust labels on untrusted context.

**Why / background.** The conductor owns the instruction hierarchy. Repo files,
comments, tool output, and scout findings are UNTRUSTED data — they may inform
implementation but must never override the Slice contract, safety policy, locked
tests, AGENTS.md, or Conveyor rules. PromptBuilder must label untrusted excerpts
explicitly so RunCheck can later reject outputs that followed them.

**Scope.** versioned prompt envelope (Role, Autonomy, Project Instructions, Slice
Contract, Context Pack w/ untrusted-data banner, Safety Policy, Work Rules,
Required Verification, Required Output Schema); InstructionSource trust labeling;
snapshot tests; prompt immutability + template version recording.

**Acceptance.** Snapshot-tested; no unlocked fields omitted; untrusted sections
carry explicit trust banners.

**Refs.** §5, §14, §22.4 (9), §25 P1.6. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: PromptBuilder envelope, trust-labeled InstructionSource rows, structured output schema, immutability, and snapshot coverage are implemented, verified, committed, and pushed.

title: P1.6.1 PromptBuilder envelope + InstructionSource trust labels
description:
**What.** PromptBuilder versioned prompt envelope + InstructionSource trust labels.
**Why.** The conductor owns the instruction hierarchy; untrusted repo/tool data
must be labeled so it cannot override the contract/policy/locked tests.
**Scope.** envelope sections (§14: Role, Autonomy, Project Instructions, Slice
Contract, Context Pack + untrusted-data banner, Safety Policy, Work Rules,
Required Verification, Required Output Schema); InstructionSource records
(trust_level + digest + included_in_prompt); template_version.
**Acceptance.** Untrusted sections carry explicit trust banners; InstructionSource
rows recorded for each input.
**Refs.** §5, §14.
close_reason: Implemented PromptBuilder envelope assembly with required sections and persisted trust-labeled InstructionSource rows for template, project, plan, brief, AGENTS.md, context pack, repo files, and tool outputs.

title: P1.6.2 Output schema + prompt immutability + snapshot tests
description:
**What.** Output-schema section + prompt immutability + snapshot tests.
**Why.** Prompts are immutable artifacts; template versions are recorded so later
learning compares outcomes across revisions.
**Scope.** required output schema (summary/files_changed/commands_attempted/
acceptance_mapping/known_risks/blocker?); persist RunPrompt immutably; snapshot
tests asserting no unlocked fields omitted.
**Acceptance.** Snapshot-tested; prompt body is content-addressed + immutable.
**Refs.** §14, §23.
close_reason: Added structured required output schema, RunPrompt body_sha256 content address, create/read-only prompt resource, DB immutability trigger, and prompt snapshot/immutability tests.

title: P1.7 — Docker workspace + policy + baseline/calibration
description:
# P1.7 — Docker workspace + policy enforcement + baseline/calibration

**What.** The `SandboxRunner` behaviour with a hardened Docker adapter, workspace
materialization + cleanup + reaper, the CredentialBroker/CredentialLease,
conductor-network segregation, end-to-end implement-profile enforcement, and the
BaselineHealth + AcceptanceCalibration stations.

**Why / background.** Docker is necessary but not sufficient. Sandbox constraints
must remain true even if the agent ignores instructions (rootless, no socket, no
host home, network=none by default, no-new-privs, resource limits). The
conductor DB/ledger must be UNREACHABLE from the sandbox network so a
compromised/injected agent cannot probe or mutate conductor state. Baseline must
be green and the locked acceptance suite must calibrate RED on the base commit
for expected reasons before implementation — otherwise the test does not prove
anything.

**Scope.** SandboxRunner behaviour + Docker adapter (materialize/exec/destroy);
sandbox hardening (§12.3); WorkspaceMaterialization lifecycle + cleanup_policy +
SandboxReaper; CredentialBroker + CredentialLease; conductor-network
segregation; implement-profile enforcement (allowed run; forbidden→incident);
BaselineHealth + AcceptanceCalibration + TestPackCalibration record.

**Acceptance.** Allowed commands run; forbidden fixtures create incidents;
baseline green on base; locked acceptance suite calibrates expected red;
unexpected greens/missing identities block before implementation.

**Refs.** §12.3, §12.4, §12.5, §22.4 (6,7,10), §25 P1.7. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED.
close_reason: All P1.7 child beads 7.1-7.7 implemented, verified, committed, and pushed.

title: P1.7.1 SandboxRunner behaviour + Docker adapter
description:
**What.** SandboxRunner behaviour + Docker adapter (materialize/exec/destroy).
**Why.** Keeps Docker from becoming the conductor API; leaves room for Podman/
Firecracker/K8s/remote sandboxes later.
**Scope.** SandboxRunner behaviour (§12.5); Docker adapter: container-per-run from
pinned image; materialize repo checkout at base commit; exec NormalizedCommand;
destroy.
**Acceptance.** Materialize→exec→destroy round-trips against the sample image.
**Refs.** §12.5, §15.
close_reason: Implemented Sandbox.Runner behavior callbacks, DockerRunner materialize/exec/destroy, Materialized handle, workspace tracking, and Docker adapter coverage.

title: P1.7.2 Sandbox hardening (rootless/no-socket/network=none/limits)
description:
**What.** Sandbox run-spec hardening.
**Why.** Sandbox constraints must remain true even if the agent ignores
instructions.
**Scope.** non-root/rootless; no privileged; no Docker socket; no host home mount;
read-only mounts for contracts/policies/.conveyor; rw only for workspace;
no-new-privileges; seccomp/AppArmor where available; CPU/mem/process/output/
wall-clock limits; network=none by default. Doctor fails hard if required
constraints unavailable for the selected profile.
**Acceptance.** A container runs with all constraints; missing required constraint
fails doctor for that profile.
**Refs.** §12.3.
close_reason: Implemented hardened Docker profile flags, wired them into DockerRunner, and added Doctor sandbox constraint checks with tests.

title: P1.7.3 WorkspaceMaterialization lifecycle + SandboxReaper
description:
**What.** WorkspaceMaterialization lifecycle + cleanup + SandboxReaper.
**Why.** Each purpose (baseline/calibration/implement/gate/canary/post_integration)
gets its own materialization; failed workspaces preserved only when policy allows;
orphans reaped.
**Scope.** materialization records + head_tree_sha256; cleanup_policy (delete/
preserve_on_failure/preserve_always) + cleanup_status; Sandbox.Reaper for orphan
containers/workspaces.
**Acceptance.** Workspaces clean up per policy; reaper removes orphans after
crash.
**Refs.** §6.1, §8 (reaper), §15 (steps 1,10,11).
notes: Bead-review revision 2026-06-17 (B2): this bead is the SOLE owner of the SandboxReaper (orphan container/workspace cleanup). iqb.14.1 and iqb.14.7 reference it; they do not rebuild it.
close_reason: Implemented workspace cleanup policy handling, head tree digests, and SandboxReaper orphan cleanup with coverage.

title: P1.7.4 CredentialBroker + CredentialLease
description:
**What.** CredentialBroker + CredentialLease.
**Why.** Pi provides no built-in credential controls; the broker owns provider
access. Raw secrets should not be injected unless no safer adapter mode exists.
**Scope.** short-lived leases scoped to one run/station; exposed only as named env
keys allowed by policy; never written to prompts/artifacts/child logs; revoked on
cancel/violation/completion.
**Acceptance.** A lease is scoped + expiring; revoked on completion; secrets never
appear in artifacts.
**Refs.** §12.4, §12.5.
close_reason: Implemented CredentialBroker issue/revoke/expire lifecycle with policy env-key validation and non-persistent secret handling.

title: P1.7.5 Conductor-network segregation (DB unreachable from sandbox)
description:
**What.** Conductor-network segregation.
**Why.** A compromised/injected agent must not be able to probe or mutate
conductor state. The AshPostgres DB, ledger, and internal services must be
UNREACHABLE from the sandbox network; approved egress is brokered to external
hosts only.
**Scope.** network policy per station (§12.4 table); egress proxy/allowlist;
assert conductor DB unreachable from sandbox.
**Acceptance.** From inside the sandbox, the conductor DB/ledger are unreachable;
only allowlisted egress works.
**Refs.** §12.0 (threats), §12.4.
close_reason: Implemented sandbox network policy defaults, internal egress allowlist rejection, and Docker profile host-network safeguards.

title: P1.7.6 Implement-profile enforcement end-to-end
description:
**What.** End-to-end implement-profile enforcement.
**Why.** Prove the policy engine + sandbox + ToolExecutor compose: allowed
commands run; forbidden ones create incidents.
**Scope.** wire ToolExecutor (P0.6) through the Docker SandboxRunner under the
implement profile; allowed-command happy path; forbidden-command fixtures →
Incident + block.
**Acceptance.** Allowed commands run; forbidden fixtures create incidents and stop
the run.
**Refs.** §12.5, §25 P1.7.
close_reason: Implemented policy-bound sandbox executor that routes allowed commands through DockerRunner and blocks forbidden commands before sandbox execution.

title: P1.7.7 BaselineHealth + AcceptanceCalibration stations
description:
**What.** BaselineHealth + AcceptanceCalibration stations + TestPackCalibration.
**Why.** Stations 6–7. Baseline must be green on the base commit; the locked
acceptance suite must calibrate RED for the expected reasons BEFORE implementation
— otherwise a test proves nothing. Unexpected greens / missing identities /
unrelated failures block the Slice before implementation.
**Scope.** BaselineHealth (clean materialization, baseline_regression suites);
AcceptanceCalibration (locked acceptance_locked TestPack on base, expected red);
TestPackCalibration record (expected_failures/unexpected_passes/unexpected_failures,
status valid/invalid).
**Acceptance.** Baseline green on base; acceptance suite calibrates expected red;
unexpected results block before implementation.
**Refs.** §22.4 (6,7), §17 (tests stage).
close_reason: Implemented BaselineHealth and AcceptanceCalibration stations with TestPackCalibration records and result blobs.

title: P1.8 — Pi AgentRunner over RPC (+ fake runner)
description:
# P1.8 — Pi AgentRunner over RPC (+ fake runner)

**What.** The `AgentRunner` behaviour + capability→autonomy mapping, the
normalized agent event envelope, the `Conveyor.AgentRunner.Pi` adapter (BEAM Port
over RPC/JSONL, 11-step flow §15), heartbeat/idle/timeout enforcement, PatchSet
capture, and a deterministic Fake runner for default CI + adapter conformance
tests.

**Why / background.** Pi is first because it provides a structured RPC seam and
minimal orchestration overlap, but the contract is deliberately broader so
Cursor/Codex/Claude Code/OpenHands/Aider/Goose can be swapped without reworking
the state machine. RawRunResult is the agent's REPORTED output — NOT trusted
evidence. The conductor collects the final git diff as a PatchSet (never trusts
uncommitted workspace state) and verifies on a fresh checkout. Default CI uses
the Fake runner — no live model calls.

**Scope.** AgentRunner behaviour + Capabilities + AgentProfile capability→
autonomy mapping; normalized event envelope + required event types → ledger;
Pi adapter (Port, RPC, 11-step flow, host-controlled-tools vs in-container
observe-only profiles); heartbeat/idle/timeout/output-limit + cancel; PatchSet
capture from fresh git diff; Fake AgentRunner + adapter conformance tests.

**Acceptance.** Events stream to ledger w/ monotonic sequence numbers; final diff
captured; timeouts/idle detection work; Fake runner exercises the flow with no
credential; conformance tests pass.

**Refs.** §15, §15.1, §22.4 (10), §23, §25 P1.8. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED (fake runner never-cut; live Pi tagged).
close_reason: Closed after all P1.8 child beads were implemented and verified: AgentRunner behaviour/capabilities, ledger event envelope, Pi RPC adapter, session limits/cancel, PatchSet capture, and Fake runner conformance.

title: P1.8.1 AgentRunner behaviour + Capabilities + autonomy mapping
description:
**What.** AgentRunner behaviour + Capabilities + capability→autonomy mapping.
**Why.** Keeps Pi/Cursor/Codex/Claude Code/OpenHands/Aider/Goose interchangeable.
Capabilities (pre-exec policy, cancellation, diff capture, cost reporting, mcp,
structured output, session resume, known_limitations) map to an autonomy ceiling
— explicitly, not implicitly. Negative capabilities recorded in RunSpec so old
evidence stays interpretable.
**Scope.** AgentRunner behaviour (run/cancel/capabilities); Capabilities struct
(§15); AgentProfile; capability→autonomy-ceiling mapping per the §15 matrix.
**Acceptance.** An adapter without pre-exec policy is capped at L1 (observe-only)
in a hardened sandbox; capabilities recorded in RunSpec.
**Refs.** §15.
close_reason: Implemented AgentRunner behaviour, capabilities/profile/raw-result structs, explicit capability-to-autonomy mapping, negative capability normalization, and RunSpec snapshot coverage.

title: P1.8.2 Normalized agent event envelope → ledger
description:
**What.** Normalized agent event envelope → ledger.
**Why.** Adapters may emit rich raw transcripts, but the NORMALIZED event stream
is what LiveView/RunCheck/replay/policy/conformance consume. MCP/slash-commands
are alternative transport, never a bypass of command policy or evidence capture.
**Scope.** conveyor.agent_event@1 envelope; required event types (session_started
… session_completed); monotonic sequence_no; raw_ref to blobs; write into Ledger.
**Acceptance.** All required event types stream with monotonic sequence numbers;
raw transcript content-addressed.
**Refs.** §15.1.
close_reason: Implemented normalized AgentRunner event recorder with conveyor.agent_event@1 envelope, monotonic sequence enforcement, raw blob refs, and ledger writes.

title: P1.8.3 Conveyor.AgentRunner.Pi (Port/RPC, 11-step flow)
description:
**What.** `Conveyor.AgentRunner.Pi` over RPC (BEAM Port, JSONL), 11-step flow.
**Why.** Pi is first: a structured RPC seam, no TUI scraping, minimal
orchestration overlap. Two profiles: pi_host_controlled_tools (preferred;
tools via ToolExecutor) and pi_in_container_observe_only (L1 only; stricter
sandbox).
**Scope.** §15 steps: materialize implement workspace; container from pinned image;
mount workspace + caches only; inject scoped creds + safe env; launch Pi RPC over
Port; prefer ToolExecutor routing else observe-only + lower ceiling; stream events
+ heartbeats; enforce limits; collect final git diff as PatchSet; apply to fresh
gate materialization; teardown per cleanup policy.
**Acceptance.** Given a RunPrompt, Pi edits the repo and returns a RawRunResult;
events stream; diff captured from fresh base.
**Refs.** §15, §12.5 (profiles).
close_reason: Implemented Conveyor.AgentRunner.Pi with Phase 1 profiles, JSONL RPC client seam, ledger event streaming, raw transcript blob refs, and fresh-base git diff capture.

title: P1.8.4 Heartbeat/idle/timeout/output limits + cancel
description:
**What.** Heartbeat / idle / timeout / output-limit enforcement + cancel.
**Why.** Bound runaway sessions; non-progress is budget exhaustion (ties to
RunBudget P0.6.7).
**Scope.** heartbeat tracking; max wall-clock/idle; output-size limit; cancel
(best_effort/hard per capability); emit cancel_requested/acknowledged events.
**Acceptance.** Idle/timeout/output-flood stops the session with a structured
finding; cancel is acknowledged.
**Refs.** §12.2 (budget), §15.
close_reason: Implemented AgentRunner session limits with heartbeat/idle/wall-clock/output accounting, structured stop findings, and Pi cancel_requested/cancel_acknowledged event emission.

title: P1.8.5 PatchSet capture from fresh git diff
description:
**What.** PatchSet capture from a fresh git diff.
**Why.** Do NOT trust uncommitted workspace state as evidence; the conductor
captures the final git diff against the base commit.
**Scope.** compute diff base_commit→head; PatchSet (patch_ref/sha256,
changed/added/deleted/renamed, lines_added/deleted, touches_locked_paths,
applies_cleanly); store patch as content-addressed blob.
**Acceptance.** PatchSet records scope + locked-path touch flag + clean-apply
status.
**Refs.** §6.1, §15 (step 9).
close_reason: Implemented fresh-base PatchSet capture with content-addressed patch blobs, file scope metrics, locked-path touch detection, clean-apply checks, and Pi run integration.

title: P1.8.6 Fake AgentRunner + adapter conformance tests
description:
**What.** Deterministic Fake AgentRunner + adapter conformance tests.
**Why.** Default CI must use a deterministic fake (no live model calls); the
hermetic tracer + canaries depend on it (never-cut). Conformance tests validate
every adapter.
**Scope.** Fake runner returning canned RawRunResult/patch; adapter conformance
suite (capability reporting, startup, normalized event streaming, monotonic
sequence, cancellation, timeout, diff capture, malformed-output handling, policy
semantics).
**Acceptance.** Fake runner drives the full flow with no credential; conformance
suite passes for Fake (and is ready for Pi).
**Refs.** §15.1, §18 (adapter_conformance), §23.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic Fake AgentRunner with canned RawRunResult/PatchSet flow and reusable adapter conformance tests for capabilities, event streaming, monotonic sequence, cancellation, diff capture, and malformed-output findings.

title: P1.9 — Evidence recorder + independent verification
description:
# P1.9 — Evidence recorder + independent verification

**What.** `EvidenceRecorder` applies the recorded PatchSet to a CLEAN gate
workspace, independently re-runs verification suites from structured test results,
maps acceptance criteria to evidence, writes evidence/dossier/manifest/diff/logs
idempotently, and runs the Security.Redactor.

**Why / background.** Design law 3: no completion without evidence — agent
self-report is not evidence. The conductor independently records it. The
clean-container reproducibility law: a diff that passes in the agent container
must pass in the gate container or the run fails. Redaction must preserve
provenance semantics (raw vs redacted digests) and never project sensitive blobs.

**Scope.** apply PatchSet to clean gate workspace; independent rerun via test
result adapters (JUnit/TAP/JSON); AC→evidence mapping + reject missing required
tests; evidence/dossier/manifest/diff/logs write + idempotency; Security.Redactor
(scan + sensitivity classification + quarantine); clean-container reproducibility
test.

**Acceptance.** Writes the full evidence set; maps ACs; rejects missing required
tests; regeneration is idempotent; secrets are detected and blocked/redacted per
policy.

**Refs.** §5, §16, §16.1, §22.4 (11), §23, §25 P1.9. Cutline: TRACER_REQUIRED (clean-gate verification never-cut).
