Verified with Elixir 1.20: focused artifact/tool/station tests (15 passed), full gate (175 passed), credo --strict clean, dialyzer passed with existing ignored warning count.

title: P0.8.3 Idempotent run-directory projection
description:
**What.** Idempotent run-directory projection.
**Why.** Projection regeneration must be idempotent and must verify blobs FIRST,
never trusting a projection path as input.
**Scope.** write .conveyor/runs/<run_attempt_id>/ (manifest/dossier/evidence/
review/gate/diff/commands/codescent/canary) from DB records + blobs; stable
ordering; exclude non-deterministic fields from digests.
**Acceptance.** Same record → same tree + checksums on regeneration.
**Refs.** §6.4.
close_reason: Made local run projection deterministic by canonicalizing manifest/checksum JSON and added idempotence coverage for stable ordering, stale-file removal, identical tree snapshots, and stable checksums across regeneration. Verified with Elixir 1.20: focused projector tests (4 passed), full gate (176 passed), credo --strict clean, dialyzer passed with existing ignored warning count.

title: P0.8.4 RunBundle manifest + bundle_root_sha256
description:
**What.** RunBundle manifest + bundle_root_sha256.
**Why.** The run directory gets a single canonical identity (Conveyor's first
trust anchor) so every PR body, dossier, gate result, and approval references one
digest.
**Scope.** conveyor.run_bundle@1 manifest (entries w/ path/kind/sha256/size/
sensitivity/schema_version); bundle_root_sha256 = sha256(canonical entries),
excluding timestamps/host paths/non-deterministic ordering.
**Acceptance.** Identical runs produce identical bundle_root_sha256; excluded
fields don't affect the digest.
**Refs.** §6.4.
close_reason: Implemented conveyor.run_bundle@1 manifest shape with deterministic entries and bundle_root_sha256 derived from canonical entry fields. Verified manifest digest/root digest in projector tests. Verified with Elixir 1.20: focused projector tests (4 passed), full gate (176 passed), credo --strict clean, dialyzer passed with existing ignored warning count.

title: P0.8.5 Artifact resource integration (sensitivity + identity)
description:
**What.** Artifact resource integration (kind/sensitivity/schema_version + path vs
identity).
**Why.** Tie projected files to Artifact rows; enforce that sensitive/quarantined
blobs are never projected.
**Scope.** create Artifact rows on projection; sensitivity gating (public/internal/
sensitive/redacted/quarantined); record producer/subject_kind/schema_version.
**Acceptance.** Sensitive blobs are not written to run dirs; every projected file
has an Artifact row + digest.
**Refs.** §6.1, §6.4, §16 (redaction).
close_reason: Enforced artifact projection sensitivity gating: sensitive and quarantined artifacts are excluded from run directories and manifests, while public/internal/redacted entries remain tied to Artifact rows by projection path and sha256 identity. Verified with Elixir 1.20: focused projector tests (5 passed), full gate (177 passed), credo --strict clean, dialyzer passed with existing ignored warning count.

title: P0.8.6 mix conveyor.report
description:
**What.** `mix conveyor.report RUN_ATTEMPT_ID`.
**Why.** Regenerate dossier/manifest/evidence/review/gate/diff/pr_body so the
report is useful even outside LiveView (headless-first).
**Scope.** CLI invoking the projector; print summary; emit machine-readable paths.
**Acceptance.** Regenerates all artifacts for a run attempt idempotently.
**Refs.** §9.3.
close_reason: Added mix conveyor.report RUN_ATTEMPT_ID with blob/projection root options, projector regeneration, and JSON output containing projection_path, manifest_path, entry_paths, artifact_count, manifest_sha256, and bundle_root_sha256. Verified idempotent report regeneration. Verified with Elixir 1.20: focused report/replay tests (3 passed), full gate (178 passed), credo --strict clean, dialyzer passed with existing ignored warning count.

title: SCRATCH-PROBE child
close_reason: Tracker scratch/probe artifact created by probe; dependent scratch bead is closed and no repository deliverable is specified.

title: SCRATCH-PROBE child2 dep
close_reason: Tracker scratch/probe artifact created by probe with no description, acceptance criteria, files, or repository deliverable; closing as non-deliverable cleanup before proceeding with scoped implementation beads.

title: Future roadmap (deferred)
description:
# Future roadmap (deferred)

Container for the deliberately-deferred roadmap. These stubs exist so the trail
from the Phase-0/1 tracer bullet to the long-term autonomous factory is visible
in the tracker WITHOUT cluttering ready work. Phase 0/1 seeds the data model and
evidence fields these phases need; do NOT build them now.

**Refs.** PHASE-0-1 plan §6.2 (deferred resources), §26 (deferred roadmap hooks).

title: (DEFER) Phase 2 — Decomposition + approval gate
description:
# (DEFER) Phase 2 — Decomposition + approval gate

Spec agent converts a handoff-ready plan into Epics/Slices/Briefs; critic agent
audits contracts; human approves/tweaks the Slice breakdown before execution. The
plan compiler graduates from audit to GENERATION, but audit remains the gate.
Phase 0/1 already builds the audit + traceability seam this depends on.

**Do not build now.** Documented future hook only. **Refs.** §26 Phase 2.
close_reason: Superseded by the active Phase 1.5+2 program epic software-factory-ai-aamg (Phase-2 increments software-factory-ai-aamg.4 P2-A Compiler Core, software-factory-ai-aamg.5 P2-B Contract Foundry). This one-line roadmap placeholder is replaced by the detailed bead tree.

title: (DEFER) Phase 3 — Parallel fleet + merge queue
description:
# (DEFER) Phase 3 — Parallel fleet + merge queue

Dispatcher selects ready Slices; WorkerPool runs isolated containers
concurrently; MergeQueue integrates into dev then a phase gate promotes to main.
Conflict domains + likely_files (captured in Phase 1) feed scheduling. Earns its
place ONLY after the single-Slice loop proves gate honesty + adapter stability.

**Do not build now.** **Refs.** §26 Phase 3, §27 (swarm-readiness fields).

title: (DEFER) Phase 4 — Verification pyramid
description:
# (DEFER) Phase 4 — Verification pyramid

Slice gate stays fast; Epic gate adds integration/e2e, property + mutation tests,
adversarial red-team review; Phase gate adds full regression, dependency/security
audit, and a human digest. The Phase-1 gate stage model is already designed to
support epic/phase gates.

**Do not build now.** **Refs.** §26 Phase 4, §17.

title: (DEFER) Phase 5 — Autonomy + self-healing
description:
# (DEFER) Phase 5 — Autonomy + self-healing

Watchdog detects silence/loops/repeated failures/no-progress; retry budgets
escalate to a supervisor agent, re-plan, park, or stop-the-line. Autonomy level
rises ONLY after measured gate reliability (design law 4). Phase 1 records the
RunBudget + failure-taxonomy data this needs.

**Do not build now.** **Refs.** §26 Phase 5, §12.2 (budget), §20.

title: (DEFER) Phase 6 — Economic governor + observability
description:
# (DEFER) Phase 6 — Economic governor + observability

Cost ledger, budget caps, rate-limit-aware credentials, runaway kill-switch;
LiveView cost meters + critical path + parked queue; object-store artifact backend
(S3/R2) behind the Projector behaviour + cross-run analytics (e.g. DuckDB over the
evidence blobs). Phase 1 ships the Projector seam + bounded metrics for this.

**Do not build now.** **Refs.** §26 Phase 6, §6.4, §8.1.

title: (DEFER) Phase 7 — Learning loop
description:
# (DEFER) Phase 7 — Learning loop

Structured memory + pgvector recall; prompt-template optimization based on
first-pass success / rework rounds / context-pack misses / review rejection / cost
per success; factory retrospective proposes AGENTS.md, policy, plan-template, and
prompt-template updates. Phase 1 records prompt template versions + retrospective
data this needs.

**Do not build now.** **Refs.** §26 Phase 7.

title: (DEFER) Phase 8 — Throughput upgrades
description:
# (DEFER) Phase 8 — Throughput upgrades

Interface-stub parallelism; swarm dry-run simulator; agent reputation routing;
conflict heatmap; BEAM distribution for horizontal scale; optional adapters to
Beads, GitHub Issues, Linear, OpenHands, Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Aider,
Goose. The AgentRunner behaviour + ExternalTaskRef spec keep these seams open.

**Do not build now.** **Refs.** §26 Phase 8, §15.

title: (DEFER) Deferred Ash resources (schema specs only)
description:
# (DEFER) Deferred Ash resources (schema specs only)

These resources have documented schema sketches, invariants, and expected event
types under docs/future-schemas/ (see P0.3.14) but NO migrations until a phase
uses them (promotion rule §6.2): WorkspacePool (pooled/warm workspaces), TaskClaim
(multi-agent claim semantics), MergeQueueItem (dev/main integration queue),
BudgetLedger (economic governor beyond Phase-1 RunBudget), AgentReputation
(empirical model/adapter routing), Memory (pgvector institutional recall),
ExternalTaskRef (adapter to Beads/GitHub Issues/Linear).

**Do not build now.** **Refs.** §6.2.
